judgments.comparative_verdict: Both cover (a)-(c) equally well; Report 2 edges ahead by explicitly tying the fixed conftest RESULTS_DB path to parallel-run collision and prescribing an override, satisfying (d) where Report 1 hedges.

report_1.evidence: pipeline/registry.py collision: F-02, F-05, F-09 are three different one-line edits within lines 3-11 of the same small file. ... but still merge-conflict-prone if done as three separate branches instead of one.

report_2.evidence: Engineer A — pipeline/registry.py (F-02 add aurora-1 endpoint mapping, F-05 bump DEFAULT_RUBRIC_VERSION to rubric-v3, F-09 fix DEFAULT_DATASET path). Three independent constants in one small file; trivial, no logic branching, safe as one owner doing three small commits.

report_1.evidence: Groups B and D have zero dependencies and can start immediately, in parallel, by anyone. ... Group C's second half (F-11) should not start until Group C's first half (F-01) lands

report_2.evidence: Engineers A, B, C, and D can all start in wave 1, in parallel — their target files (registry.py, rubric.py+new test file, run_batch.py, the two prompt files) are fully disjoint

report_1.evidence: F-06 ... is a scoring-policy decision, not a bug with an objectively correct fix ... picking new numbers changes how every past and future graded answer compares. ... This should go back to whoever owns the grading rubric/quality bar for a target value before it becomes an engineering task.

report_2.evidence: F-06 changes actual scoring output (PARTIAL_CREDIT_THRESHOLDS), which is user/behavior-facing, not a mechanical bug. ... that's a behavior change needing sign-off from whoever owns the rubric/scoring policy ... Escalate for a decision on the actual target thresholds before it's implemented.

Awareness that RESULTS_DB (tests/conftest.py) defaults to /var/tmp/llm-scoring/results.db — if any test path actually touches that DB ..., engineers running test suites in parallel should override RESULTS_DB to their own scratch path so parallel runs don't collide
